title: Connections, Channel Ownership, Heartbeats, and Recovery · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A Go pairing room incrementally implements a cancellable connection and channel recovery state machine, first with deterministic values and then with a changed failure, while keeping prior messaging invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a cancellable connection and channel recovery state machine while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use bounded long-lived AMQP connections and explain multiplexed channels, negotiated limits, names, TLS, and lifecycle cost; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Assign channel ownership by publisher or consumer lifecycle and prevent concurrent confirm, acknowledgement, and topology operations from corrupting assumptions; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Choose a heartbeat timeout with network and flow-control evidence and distinguish heartbeat interval, TCP keepalive, and false-positive failure; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Long Lived Connection
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go messaging team handles case 4040 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound heartbeat and recovery delay while rejecting stale channel-generation notifications; keep credentials and connection URLs out of output; then remove the parking binding and expose the remediation gap. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use bounded long-lived AMQP connections and explain multiplexed channels, negotiated limits, names, TLS, and lifecycle cost; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Long Lived Connection.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use bounded long-lived amqp connections and explain multiplexed channels, negotiated limits, names, tls, and lifecycle cost; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Long Lived Connection, keep Long Lived Connection connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

workshop case — A workshop Go messaging team handles case 7750 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Recovery Order Generation condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.
